headline: Citibank Announces $2 Billion Redemption of 5.438% Notes Due 2026 and $1 Billion Redemption of Floating Rate Notes Due 2026
summary: NEW YORK, March 25, 2026--Citibank, N.A. is announcing the redemption, in whole, constituting $2,000,000,000 of its 5.438% Notes due 2026 (the "fixed rate notes") (ISIN: US17325FBF45) and $1,000,000,000 of its Floating Rate Notes due 2026 (the "floating rate notes" and together with the fixed rate notes, the "notes") (ISIN: US17325FBE79).

headline: How The Pearson (LSE:PSON) Narrative Is Shifting As Analysts Rework Targets And Growth Assumptions
summary: Analysts have been adjusting price targets on Pearson, with moves such as a cut to $13.90 and a new 1,300 GBp target helping reset expectations around what investors might be willing to pay. These changes sit alongside mixed commentary, where some analysts are more cautious on revenue while others view the recent AI driven selloff as creating a more appealing entry point. Read on to see how these shifting targets fit into the broader analyst narrative and how you can track it over time. Stay...

headline: How The H & M (OM:HM B) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Fair Value Signals
summary: The fair value estimate for H & M Hennes & Mauritz has been adjusted to SEK 163.42 from SEK 162.62, a small move that still matters if you are watching entry and exit levels closely. This shift sits against mixed Street research, with some analysts lifting targets into the mid SEK 170s while others sit closer to the mid SEK 160s, reflecting different views on execution risk and how much is already priced in. Read on to see what is driving these changes and how you can keep track of the...

headline: Citigroup (C) Is Up 5.6% After BlackRock ETF Mandate And Bond Issuances - Has The Bull Case Changed?

These capital-raising moves and high-profile conference appearances come alongside Citigroup’s appointment by BlackRock to support middle-office functions for trillions in ETF...

headline: How The Whitbread (LSE:WTB) Investment Narrative Is Shifting With New Targets And Buybacks
summary: Whitbread’s central price target has shifted to £2,600, alongside only a very small move in the modelled fair value per share from £28.85 to £28.79. Recent research shows some firms lifting targets by £100 to £200, while another trims and moves to an Equal Weight stance at £2,600. This leaves you with a mixed but useful set of signals to interpret. Next, you will see how to read these changing targets and keep track of the evolving analyst story around the shares.
